title: Prose-heal tier ships: --prose-heal drafts a reconciled overview on adopted copies; deterministic corrections stay authoritative
context: Shipped from the roadmap (the last live item). Adopted copies healed deterministically but the stale prose stayed the only narrative — readers had corrections, not a rewrite.
decision: `hunch wiki --prose-heal` (requires the LLM; refused with --no-llm) adds a '🩹 Reconciled overview' under the adopted copy's banner: SynthProvider.draftProse over adoptProsePrompt (stale doc content + the CURRENT decision per pinned topic; 100-180 words, dec-id citations, never invents). Same doctrine as page prose: output is never hashed, any failure degrades to the byte-identical deterministic copy, and the graph-correction callouts below remain the authoritative layer. Note the heal semantics: --heal only regenerates STALE copies, so adding prose to fresh copies takes a full `hunch wiki --prose-heal` run — prose is garnish, not a freshness input, by design. Dogfooded live on the overlay's adopted README/blog copies.
alternatives_rejected: LLM-rewriting the copy's body in place: nondeterministic bytes as the primary content would gut the tamper tripwire's meaning and the deterministic-copy guarantee — the overview is ADDITIVE
Hashing the prose so --heal refreshes it: nondeterministic output would make every copy permanently stale (rejected once already for page prose)
